<!-- markdownlint-disable-file -->

# Rust Clean Code — Examples by Rule

The catalog in `SKILL.md` is the index; the worked examples below demonstrate each rule.
Reproduce these patterns when fixing or editing Rust code, and cite the matching rule ID.

---

## Comments (C1-C5)

### C1: No Inappropriate Information

Comments should not hold metadata. Use Git for author names, change history,
ticket numbers, and dates.

```rust
// Bad
// Author: Jane Doe
// Changed: 2024-01-15 — added null check for ticket AB#1234
pub fn find_user(id: &str) -> Option<User> { todo!() }

// Good — technical context only
// Binary search requires the slice to be sorted before calling.
pub fn find<T: Ord>(sorted: &[T], target: &T) -> Option<usize> { todo!() }
```

### C2: Delete Obsolete Comments

If a comment describes code that no longer exists or behaves differently,
delete it immediately. Stale comments become sources of confusion.

### C3: No Redundant Comments

```rust
// Bad — the code already says this
i += 1; // increment i
user.save().await?; // save the user

// Good — explains WHY, not WHAT
i += 1; // compensate for zero-indexing in the display layer
```

### C4: Write Comments Well

Use `///` doc comments for public API items. Document intent and non-obvious
behavior. Include `# Examples`, `# Errors`, and `# Panics` sections.

```rust
// Bad — restates the signature
/// Get user by id.
pub async fn get_user(id: &str) -> Result<Option<User>> { todo!() }

// Good — documents intent, edge cases, and error variants
/// Retrieves a user by their unique identifier.
///
/// Returns `Ok(None)` when no user with `id` exists rather than an error,
/// so callers can distinguish "not found" from an I/O failure.
///
/// # Errors
///
/// Returns [`ServiceError::InvalidInput`] when `id` is empty.
/// Returns [`ServiceError::Io`] on database I/O failure.
///
/// # Examples
///
/// ```
/// let user = service.get_user("user-42").await?;
/// assert!(user.is_some());
/// ```
pub async fn get_user(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<User>> {
    if id.is_empty() {
        return Err(ServiceError::invalid_input("id must not be empty"));
    }
    self.repository.find_by_id(id).await
}
```

### C5: Never Commit Commented-Out Code

```rust
// DELETE THIS — it's an abomination
// fn old_calculate_tax(income: f64) -> f64 {
//     income * 0.15
// }
```

Git remembers everything. Delete it.

---

## Environment (E1-E2)

### E1: One Command to Build

```bash
cargo build
```

### E2: One Command to Test

```bash
cargo test
```

---

## Functions (F1-F4)

### F1: Too Many Arguments (Maximum 3)

```rust
// Bad — too many parameters
pub fn create_user(
    name: &str, email: &str, age: u32,
    country: &str, timezone: &str, newsletter: bool,
) -> User { todo!() }

// Good — use a struct
pub struct CreateUserRequest {
    pub name: String,
    pub email: String,
    pub age: u32,
    pub country: String,
    pub timezone: String,
    pub newsletter: bool,
}

pub fn create_user(request: CreateUserRequest) -> User { todo!() }
```

### F2: No Output Arguments

Don't mutate arguments passed by `&mut` at public boundaries. Return owned values instead.

```rust
// Bad — mutates caller's buffer as a side effect
pub fn append_footer(report: &mut String) {
    report.push_str("\n---\nGenerated by System");
}

// Good — return the extended value
pub fn append_footer(report: &str) -> String {
    format!("{report}\n---\nGenerated by System")
}
```

### F3: No Flag Arguments

Flag arguments reveal that a function does two things. Split them.

```rust
// Bad
pub fn render(content: &str, is_mobile: bool) -> String { todo!() }

// Good
pub fn render_for_desktop(content: &str) -> String { todo!() }
pub fn render_for_mobile(content: &str) -> String { todo!() }
```

### F4: Delete Dead Functions

Functions that are never called and are not part of the public API should be deleted.
If they might be needed later, Git remembers them.

---

## General (G1-G36)

### G5: DRY — No Duplication

```rust
// Bad — same tax calculation repeated
fn calculate_invoice_total(subtotal: f64) -> f64 {
    subtotal + subtotal * 0.0825
}

fn calculate_receipt_total(subtotal: f64) -> f64 {
    subtotal + subtotal * 0.0825
}

// Good — extracted constant and shared helper
const TAX_RATE: f64 = 0.0825;

fn add_tax(subtotal: f64) -> f64 {
    subtotal * (1.0 + TAX_RATE)
}

fn calculate_invoice_total(subtotal: f64) -> f64 { add_tax(subtotal) }
fn calculate_receipt_total(subtotal: f64) -> f64 { add_tax(subtotal) }
```

### G9: Delete Dead Code

Remove unused `use` declarations, dead functions, unreachable match arms, and
unused struct fields. Run `cargo clippy` — it surfaces all of these with specific
lint names (`dead_code`, `unused_imports`, `unused_variables`).

### G16: No Obscured Intent

```rust
// Bad — what is this doing?
let r: f64 = d.iter().filter(|&&x| x == 1).map(|x| x * 1.0825).sum();

// Good — obvious at a glance
let taxable_items = orders.iter().filter(|o| o.status == OrderStatus::Taxable);
let total_with_tax: f64 = taxable_items.map(|o| o.price * (1.0 + TAX_RATE)).sum();
```

### G19: Use Explanatory Variables

```rust
// Bad
if user.age >= 18 && user.country == "US" && !user.is_banned {

// Good
let is_eligible = user.age >= 18 && user.country == "US" && !user.is_banned;
if is_eligible {
```

### G23: Prefer Traits + Dispatch over Match on Type Tags

```rust
// Bad — grows with every new type; violates open/closed
fn calculate_shipping(kind: &str, weight: f64) -> f64 {
    match kind {
        "standard" => weight * 0.5,
        "express"  => weight * 1.5,
        "overnight" => weight * 3.0,
        _ => panic!("unknown shipping kind"),
    }
}

// Good — each strategy is closed for modification
pub trait ShippingStrategy: Send + Sync {
    fn calculate(&self, weight: f64) -> f64;
}

pub struct StandardShipping;
pub struct ExpressShipping;

impl ShippingStrategy for StandardShipping {
    fn calculate(&self, weight: f64) -> f64 { weight * 0.5 }
}

impl ShippingStrategy for ExpressShipping {
    fn calculate(&self, weight: f64) -> f64 { weight * 1.5 }
}
```

### G25: Named Constants, Not Magic Numbers

```rust
// Bad
let total = subtotal * 1.0825;
if retries > 3 {
    return Err(ServiceError::invalid_input("too many retries"));
}

// Good
const TAX_RATE: f64 = 0.0825;
const MAX_RETRIES: u32 = 3;

let total = subtotal * (1.0 + TAX_RATE);
if retries > MAX_RETRIES {
    return Err(ServiceError::invalid_input("too many retries"));
}
```

### G28: Encapsulate Conditionals

```rust
// Bad
if user.age >= 18 && !user.is_banned && user.has_verified_email {

// Good
if user.is_eligible() {

// In the User impl:
pub fn is_eligible(&self) -> bool {
    self.age >= 18 && !self.is_banned && self.has_verified_email
}
```

### G29: Avoid Negative Conditionals

```rust
// Bad
if !is_not_ready { }

// Good
if is_ready { }
```

### G30: Functions Do One Thing

```rust
// Bad — validates, maps, saves, and sends notification
pub async fn register_user(&self, req: RegisterRequest) -> Result<User> {
    if req.email.is_empty() {
        return Err(ServiceError::invalid_input("email is required"));
    }
    let user = User { name: req.name, email: req.email };
    self.repo.add(&user).await?;
    self.notify.send_welcome(&user.email).await?;
    Ok(user)
}

// Good — each step named and isolated
pub async fn register_user(&self, req: RegisterRequest) -> Result<User> {
    self.validate_request(&req)?;
    let user = Self::map_to_user(req);
    self.repo.add(&user).await?;
    self.notify.send_welcome(&user.email).await?;
    Ok(user)
}
```

### G36: Law of Demeter — No Train Wrecks

```rust
// Bad
let city = order.customer.address.city.to_uppercase();

// Good — ask the object for what you need
let city = order.customer_city().to_uppercase();
```

---

## Rust-Specific (RS1-RS5)

### RS1: Module Structure

```rust
// Bad — logic crammed into lib.rs
// lib.rs — 400+ lines of mixed concerns
pub struct Config { ... }
pub struct UserService { ... }
pub fn process() { ... }

// Good — thin entry points, domain-organized modules
// lib.rs
pub mod config;
pub mod error;
pub mod services;

pub use error::{Result, ServiceError};
pub use services::UserService;

// services/user.rs — owns all user logic
use crate::{config::AppConfig, error::Result, models::User};

pub struct UserService { ... }
```

Co-locate unit tests at the bottom of the file they test:

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn given_valid_id_find_returns_user() { ... }
}
```

### RS2: Error Handling

```rust
// Bad — raw `unwrap` in a handler path
pub fn get_price(id: &str) -> f64 {
    let item = db.find(id).unwrap();   // panics on None or Err
    item.price
}

// Bad — anyhow in library code loses type information
pub fn parse(raw: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Config> { ... }

// Good — thiserror + module Result alias + ? propagation
use thiserror::Error;

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ServiceError>;

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum ServiceError {
    #[error("Not found: {message}")]
    NotFound { message: String },

    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
}

pub async fn get_price(&self, id: &str) -> Result<f64> {
    let item = self.repo.find(id).await?;  // ? propagates cleanly
    let Some(item) = item else {
        return Err(ServiceError::NotFound { message: format!("item {id}") });
    };
    Ok(item.price)
}
```

`expect()` with a message is acceptable only at initialization:

```rust
// Acceptable — startup path, runs once before serving requests
let config = AppConfig::from_env()
    .expect("AppConfig must be valid at startup");
```

### RS3: Doc Comments

```rust
// Bad — missing docs on public item
pub fn process(input: &[u8], config: &ProcessConfig) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
    todo!()
}

// Good
/// Processes raw input bytes according to the provided configuration.
///
/// # Arguments
///
/// * `input` - Raw bytes to process; must not be empty.
/// * `config` - Processing parameters including encoding and compression.
///
/// # Returns
///
/// The processed output as a byte vector.
///
/// # Errors
///
/// Returns [`ServiceError::InvalidInput`] when `input` is empty.
///
/// # Examples
///
/// ```
/// let result = process(&[0x01, 0x02], &ProcessConfig::default())?;
/// assert!(!result.is_empty());
/// ```
pub fn process(input: &[u8], config: &ProcessConfig) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
    if input.is_empty() {
        return Err(ServiceError::invalid_input("input must not be empty"));
    }
    // ...
    todo!()
}
```

Use `//!` for module-level documentation:

```rust
//! User management services.
//!
//! This module provides [`UserService`] for CRUD operations on user accounts.
//! All operations are async and backed by a [`Repository`] trait implementation.
```

### RS4: Naming Conventions

```rust
// Bad — wrong case, Hungarian notation, unclear suffix
fn ProcessData(d: Vec<u8>) -> Vec<u8> { todo!() }   // PascalCase on fn
struct userData { name: String }                    // camelCase on struct
const maxRetries: u32 = 3;                          // camelCase on const
struct RepositoryImpl { ... }                       // unhelpful suffix

// Good
fn process_data(data: Vec<u8>) -> Vec<u8> { todo!() }
struct UserData { name: String }
const MAX_RETRIES: u32 = 3;
struct UserRepository { ... }                       // *Config, *Builder, *Error suffixes
```

### RS5: Async Patterns

```rust
// Bad — blocking the Tokio runtime with synchronous I/O
async fn fetch_all(ids: &[&str]) -> Result<Vec<Item>> {
    ids.iter()
        .map(|id| std::fs::read_to_string(id))  // blocks the thread
        .collect::<std::result::Result<Vec<_>, _>>()
        .map_err(ServiceError::Io)
}

// Bad — panic in async context
async fn get(id: &str) -> Item {
    repository.find(id).await.unwrap()
}

// Good — non-blocking reads + ? propagation
async fn fetch_all(ids: &[&str]) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
    let futures: Vec<_> = ids.iter()
        .map(|id| tokio::fs::read_to_string(id))
        .collect();
    Ok(futures::future::try_join_all(futures).await?)
}

// Good — spawn_blocking for CPU-bound work
async fn compress(data: Vec<u8>) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || heavy_compression(data))
        .await
        .map_err(|e| ServiceError::invalid_input(e.to_string()))?
}
```

---

## Names (N1-N7)

### N1: Descriptive Names

```rust
// Bad
let d = std::time::Instant::now();
let u: Vec<User> = vec![];
let f = false;

// Good
let started_at = std::time::Instant::now();
let active_users: Vec<User> = vec![];
let is_email_verified = false;
```

### N5: Name Length Matches Scope

```rust
// Bad — cryptic in wide scope
for u in user_repository.find_all().await? {
    email_service.send_welcome(&u.email).await?;
}

// Bad — overly verbose in tight loop
for list_iteration_index in 0..items.len() { }

// Good
for user in user_repository.find_all().await? {
    email_service.send_welcome(&user.email).await?;
}

for i in 0..items.len() { }
```

### N6: No Encodings

```rust
// Bad — Hungarian notation
let str_name = String::from("Alice");
let b_is_active = true;
let i_repository = create_repo();

// Good
let name = String::from("Alice");
let is_active = true;
let repository = create_repo();
```

---

## Tests (T1-T9) and Rust Test Conventions (RT1-RT5)

### RT1: BDD-Style Test Names

```rust
// Bad — opaque name
#[test]
fn test1() { }

#[test]
fn process_data() { }

// Good — context, action, and expected outcome
#[test]
fn given_valid_input_parse_returns_config() { }

#[tokio::test]
async fn when_endpoint_unavailable_fetch_returns_error() { }
```

### RT2: mockall + wiremock

```rust
use mockall::automock;

#[automock]
pub trait Repository: Send + Sync {
    fn find_by_id(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Item>>;
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use mockall::predicate::*;

    #[test]
    fn given_existing_item_service_returns_it() {
        let mut mock = MockRepository::new();
        mock.expect_find_by_id()
            .with(eq("42"))
            .returning(|_| Ok(Some(Item { id: "42".into() })));

        let service = ItemService::new(Box::new(mock));
        let result = service.get("42").unwrap();
        assert_eq!(result.unwrap().id, "42");
    }
}
```

For async HTTP tests, use `wiremock`:

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn when_api_returns_ok_fetch_succeeds() {
    let mock_server = wiremock::MockServer::start().await;
    wiremock::Mock::given(wiremock::matchers::method("GET"))
        .respond_with(wiremock::ResponseTemplate::new(200)
            .set_body_string(r#"{"id":"1"}"#))
        .mount(&mock_server)
        .await;

    let client = reqwest::Client::new();
    let resp = client.get(mock_server.uri()).send().await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(resp.status(), 200);
}
```

### RT3: Test Co-location and Fixture Helpers

```rust
// Unit tests at the bottom of the same file
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    // Fixture helper — never repeat construction inline across tests
    fn sample_config() -> AppConfig {
        AppConfig {
            endpoint: "https://example.com".into(),
            polling_interval_secs: 10,
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn given_custom_interval_config_overrides_default() {
        let config = AppConfig {
            polling_interval_secs: 30,
            ..sample_config()
        };
        assert_eq!(config.polling_interval_secs, 30);
    }
}
```

### RT4: Async Tests

```rust
// Sync tests use #[test]
#[test]
fn given_empty_id_error_formats_correctly() {
    let err = ServiceError::not_found("item 42");
    assert_eq!(err.to_string(), "Not found: item 42");
}

// Async tests use #[tokio::test]
#[tokio::test]
async fn given_valid_id_get_user_returns_user() {
    let mut mock_repo = MockUserRepository::new();
    mock_repo.expect_find_by_id()
        .returning(|_| Ok(Some(User { id: "1".into(), name: "Alice".into() })));

    let service = UserService::new(Box::new(mock_repo));
    let result = service.get_user("1").await;

    assert!(result.is_ok(), "get_user should succeed for a known id");
}
```

### RT5: One Assertion Per Test

```rust
// Bad — multiple unrelated assertions
#[test]
fn user_has_correct_state() {
    let user = create_user("Alice", "alice@example.com");
    assert!(!user.id.is_empty());
    assert_eq!(user.name, "Alice");
    assert_eq!(user.email, "alice@example.com");
    assert!(!user.is_banned);
}

// Good — one behavior per test; combine only tightly related state
#[test]
fn given_valid_input_create_user_sets_name_and_email() {
    let user = create_user("Alice", "alice@example.com");
    assert_eq!(user.name, "Alice");
    assert_eq!(user.email, "alice@example.com");
}

#[test]
fn given_new_user_is_not_banned() {
    let user = create_user("Alice", "alice@example.com");
    assert!(!user.is_banned, "newly created users must not be banned");
}
```

### T5: Test Boundary Conditions

```rust
#[test]
fn given_empty_id_get_user_returns_invalid_input_error() {
    let result = block_on(service.get_user(""));
    assert!(
        matches!(result, Err(ServiceError::InvalidInput { .. })),
        "empty id should produce InvalidInput error"
    );
}

#[test]
fn given_whitespace_only_id_get_user_returns_error() {
    let result = block_on(service.get_user("   "));
    assert!(result.is_err(), "whitespace-only id must be rejected");
}
```

### T9: Tests Must Be Fast (< 100ms)

- Use `mockall` substitutes and `wiremock` mock servers for all I/O in unit tests.
- Reserve tests requiring real network or disk access for the `tests/` integration test suite.
- Run the integration suite separately with `cargo test --test '*'` and exclude from CI default runs
  when they require external infrastructure.
